repos.notes: Repo is publicly accessible at sambuko82/llm-wiki (default branch: master). Key findings: (1) The primary high-value data lives in output/products/package-readiness/ (6 JSON files covering package registry, pricing, itineraries, booking compatibility) and output/website/trust-bundle/ (10 JSON files covering products, claims, destinations, policies, people, operational data, FAQ, AEO snippets, and JSON-LD schemas). (2) output/website/schema/ contains 20+ individual tour package JSON-LD schema files per destination route (Bromo, Ijen, Tumpak Sewu, Madakaripura, Bali, student packages). (3) wiki/ directory has human-authored Markdown knowledge base organized by domain: products, credentials, destinations, finance, ops, marketing, seo, reviews, people, whatsapp, sources — all low-priority relative to compiled JSON outputs. (4) No sensitive credentials or API tokens found in file paths; wiki/credentials/ contains operational documentation (legal licenses, medical screening, police integration) — not system secrets. (5) scripts/ directory has a well-structured Python toolchain with two compiler sub-packages (compiler/ for trust-bundle, package_compiler/ for packages) and standalone scripts for inventory, analysis, DB dump, and frontmatter normalization. (6) output/finance/, output/marketing/, output/whatsapp/ directories appear empty (only .gitkeep). (7) raw/ directory contains source PDFs, GPX files, and unstructured Markdown not yet processed into clean output. Most recent activity on claims.json (2026-06-09), package-registry.json (2026-06-07)."

repos.notes:
Repository jvto-devteam/jvto-web is accessible. Key findings:

1. DATABASE: PostgreSQL via Prisma ORM. Schema has 97 models covering the full tour operator domain: packages, bookings, destinations, hotels, crew, vehicles, vendors, reviews, payments, policies, FAQs, CMS/blogs, assets, EAV content system, and NextAuth (customers/accounts/sessions tables).

2. DATA FILES: src/data/ contains static TypeScript data files (destinations, evidence registry, FAQs, knowledge base, mock data, reviews) plus structured JSON subdirectories: package-readiness/ (manifest, pricing, itineraries, registry, booking-compatibility, gap-report) and trust-bundle/ (claims, FAQ, AEO snippets, JSON-LD schemas). Blog directory has 2 markdown posts.

3. API ROUTES: Rich REST API under src/app/(api)/api/ covering all entity types. Key transactional routes: /api/checkout (POST — booking creation), /api/checkout/bank-transfer (POST), /api/booking/pay-balance (POST). Public web routes at /api/packages/web and /api/destinations/web serve the frontend. Auth via NextAuth at /api/auth/[...nextauth].

4. NOTABLE: mockData.ts is 640KB — likely a large static data dump usable for extraction. package-readiness/ and trust-bundle/ directories appear to be pre-processed clean data outputs already. EAV system (eav_entity, eav_attribute, eav_value) used for CMS/page content. knowledge_bases table stores vector embeddings (pgvector). site_identity and organization_profile models store canonical brand data.

5. NO sensitive files (PII/payment) found directly in src/data — payment data is in the DB only (booking_payment_terms, booking_payment_histories).

Repository jvto-devteam/new-backoffice is fully accessible via GitHub API. This is a Laravel backoffice application with 175 Eloquent models. Key findings: (1) A dedicated routes/data.php file contains a comprehensive /export-data/* prefix group with 60+ GET endpoints organized by domain (hotels, vehicles, crews, destinations, countries, customers, packages, include-exclude, addons, price-tiers, activities, vendors, order-channels, policies, discount, payment-methods, durations, bookings) plus a catch-all /export-data/all endpoint via ExportDataAllController. (2) Additional export endpoints exist in web.php under /rekap-hutang (finance debt recap export as PDF/Excel), /channel-revenue-report (channel revenue export), /hub (finance hub export), and /crm/export (CRM customer/country/package exports). (3) Controllers are organized under app/Http/Controllers/ExportData/ with one controller per domain. (4) Sensitive tables/models identified: users, user_logs, twt_invoice_bookings, twt_invoice_payments. Sensitive export endpoints: /export-data/customers/customers, /export-data/bookings/bookings and all booking sub-endpoints, /export-data/payment-methods/payment-methods, /crm/export/customers, /crm/export/customer-report. (5) The migration history spans from Laravel bootstrap tables through 2026-05-09, with 59 CREATE TABLE migrations found. (6) Also has ExportCSV.php and ExportSQL.php utility classes. The /export-data/all endpoint likely aggregates all data domains into a single response, which is the highest-priority endpoint for the data-core pipeline.
